items.title: Intraday post (21/Aug)
items.subtitle: Risk window is in play
items.truncated_body_text: Minutes shows that upside inflation risk as the greater concern, with tariffs a key uncertainty; the Committee wants to hold rates, only two governors (Waller and Bowman) vote for a 25 bp cut. And this is what market expects: emergency cut with hawkish guidance.

items.title: Why Sept cut odds are still high? - Intraday post (15/Aug)
items.subtitle: Debt burden, Treasury issuance, Recession odds, OpEx
items.description: Debt burden, Treasury issuance, Recession odds, OpEx
items.truncated_body_text: Core PPI come out quite hot confirming sticky inflationary pressures and tariff effects as we expected since April/May. PPI feeds into PCE more directly than CPI. Producer prices, especially trade services margins and core goods, tend to lead consumer inflation with a lag (weeks to a few months).
